en.extract:
The violaceous coucal or violet coucal (Centropus violaceus) is a cuckoo species in the family Cuculidae. It is endemic to the Bismarck Archipelago (Papua New Guinea). Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.
Due to a large range and a population that is not decreasing at a sufficient rate for immediate concern, it is classified as Least concern.

The '''violaceous coucal''' or '''violet coucal''' ('''''Centropus violaceus''''') is a [[cuckoo]] [[species]] in the [[family (biology)|family]] [[Cuculidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the [[Bismarck Archipelago]] (Papua New Guinea). Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s.

Due to a large range and a population that is not decreasing at a sufficient rate for immediate concern, it is classified as [[Least concern species|Least concern]].<ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021"/>
